summary: Iter 196 — Frontend-only validation of Phase 2 Retirement Decision OS (Retirement Control Center, ClientDecisionHub, ClientHome). 10 of 11 acceptance items PASS. The only failure is Intelligence Feed showing 1 item instead of 'at least 2' — likely because there are only 2 clients in the book so most cross-book signals collapse to a single market-impact card. Everything else (KPIs, priority clients, client list with row navigation, Decision Hub default tab + dial + 5 factors + 5 sections, live simulator scoring, what-moves-the-needle 3 actions, Opportunity Engine cards, ClientHome live what-if, and 3 regression routes) renders cleanly with no ErrorBoundary.

integration_issues.issue: Only 1 feed item rendered (acceptance asks for >=2). With only thompson_family + chen_family in the book, the buildIntelligenceFeed aggregation collapses to a single 'Market' severity-medium card. Either seed more book-wide signals (e.g. always emit a low/info 'opportunity-summary' card and a 'compliance/review-due' card) or relax the criterion. Non-blocking — feed renders, item is well-formed with severity badge.

action_items: MINOR: Make Intelligence Feed always emit at least 2 cards even on a small book (e.g. a 'Top opportunity across the book' card + the existing market card), so the feed feels alive when only 1-2 clients are loaded — needed to satisfy the 'at least 2 feed items' acceptance criterion.
critical_code_review_comments: RetirementControlCenter.jsx is clean (~210 lines), pure-derived state via useMemo, no API calls — good.

ClientDecisionHub.jsx Scenario Simulator correctly recomputes via computeReadiness with reduced numSims (150) per slider change — verified live (90 -> 54 when retire age dropped + spending raised). Performance felt acceptable.
ClientHome.jsx baseline computeReadiness uses numSims:200 and scenario uses 150 — sensible tradeoff. Live recalc on age slider verified (89 -> 83).
buildIntelligenceFeed emits only 1 card with a 2-client book; consider 1-2 'always-on' info cards (book-wide opportunity summary, NPS/satisfaction, review-due) so the feed never looks underfilled.
All required data-testids are present and stable — no flakiness during the test run.
No console errors (only standard non-blocking warnings); no ErrorBoundary triggered on any tested route.
seed_data_creation: None. All compute is client-side off CLIENT_DATA fixture (thompson_family + chen_family).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 retirement OS shipped successfully. Only outstanding item is the intelligence feed showing 1 card (vs >=2 spec). Likely a tiny tweak in /app/frontend/src/engine/bookAggregator.js to emit 1-2 always-on info cards. Score-changed verification in the simulator requires moving the retire-age slider DOWN (early retirement) or spending UP, because the test client (thompson_family) baseline is already 90/STRONG and capped at the upper bound when you push toward 'better' inputs.
rca_of_the_issue: Intelligence Feed underfilled: with only 2 high-scoring clients in CLIENT_DATA, the deterministic rules in bookAggregator.buildIntelligenceFeed only trigger the 'market movement' card. Mitigation: emit one info-tier 'book-summary' card and/or 'review-due' card unconditionally so the feed always has >=2 entries.
